7PM CBB: New Haven Vs. Chicago State

Picks:
NHC +2.5 -137 5U (Dk)
NHC TTU 64.5 -104 3U (FD)
CHS TTU 66.5 -140 3U (DK)
NHC 1H +0.5 -112 3U (FD)

7PM CBB: New Haven Vs. Chicago State

As New Haven and Chicago State get set to meet on the hardwood, both teams bring a bottom-60 squad into this one. The New Haven Colonels are 3-2 on the season and 0-1 in the Northeast conference matchups. While both of these teams are mediocre, the scale to how mediocre is extraordinarily different between these two teams. New Haven has gone 3-2 against their Northeastern Conference rivals. On the other side, Chicago State sits at 1-4 on the season and 0-1 against their conference opponents thus far.

In conference play, New Haven has posted 68 points per game while shooting 42 percent from the field and 34 percent from three-point range while averaging just 51 field goal attempts per game. They now take on a Chicago State defense surrendering just 65 points per game in conference play. They have allowed opponents to shoot 44 percent from the field while averaging 38 percent from three-point range. New Haven is scoring only 65 points per game in conference play. They have allowed opponents to shoot 44 percent from the field while averaging 38 percent from three-point range. Chicago State is scoring only 65 points per game in conference play. They have allowed opponents to shoot 44 percent from the field while averaging 38 percent from three-point range.

In the grand scheme of things, both of these teams are bottom-60 squads. However, the difference in quality between these two bottom feeders is immense. New Haven has a better offense and defense ranking just 322nd nationally in total rebound margin while Chicago State ranks even worse at 350th in the nation. Despite a New Haven breakdown.
